metadata.notes: Safe (auto-approved) path: agy has an auto-approve/permission system; the filtered transcript is DONE-only (no permission-pending status) and the parser emits NO waiting signal at all — so the safe arc ready→working→ready (never waiting, tool call fires) is observable. Driver lacks wait_turn → gap.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

**agent=yes (safe path).** agy ships an auto-classifying permission system — binary strings `Tool Permission`, `Auto-approve`, `auto-approve`, `DenyRule`/`DenyList`/`requires permission`, plus the `--dangerously-skip-permissions` flag. A benign tool call (e.g. `run_command echo hello`, or a directory listing) is auto-approved without a prompt, which is the safe arc this cell captures. (The risky arc — `working→waiting→working→ready` — is the sibling tool-gate scenario, not this one; the claudecode sibling likewise captures only the safe arc.)

**daemon=full.** The safe arc the scenario asserts is `ready → working → ready` with the invariant 'never enters waiting' plus 'at least one tool call'. This is exactly what the antigravity parser produces: `USER_INPUT` opens working; a `PLANNER_RESPONSE` WITH `tool_calls` emits an `assistant_message` + ToolUse (the tool-call requirement); the MODEL tool-result line closes it (`function_call_output`, non-settling); the empty terminal `PLANNER_RESPONSE` settles to ready. Critically, the parser has NO path that ever produces `waiting` — there is no permission-pending emission, and the FILTERED transcript.jsonl contains only `status:"DONE"` steps (no pending status), so a waiting transition is structurally impossible. The 'never waiting' invariant is therefore GUARANTEED for the safe path. The sampled transcripts confirm the `PLANNER_RESPONSE(tool_calls) → tool-result → … → empty PLANNER_RESPONSE` shape with no pending status (e.g. `9ea5200c` steps 2–3, 5–6).

**driver=gap:wait_turn.** The recipe needs `send` (tool-triggering prompt) + `wait_turn`. `wait_turn` is `not_implemented` in the scaffolded driver (`DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"`). `record` ports the agy turn-done poll before driving. NOTE: the risky/waiting branch is NOT capturable here — the filtered transcript shows no permission-pending status, so antigravity's waiting state for a tool gate would be a separate (likely incapable) concern owned by tool-gate-permission-prompt, not this safe-path cell.
caveats: Captures ONLY the safe (auto-approved) arc, like the claudecode sibling. Antigravity's parser emits NO waiting signal at all and the filtered transcript has no permission-pending status, so the risky-path waiting transition is not observable — that is the sibling tool-gate scenario's concern, not a downgrade here.
Emission UNCONFIRMED: no sample drives an interactive auto-approved tool call to completion; record confirms the tool_use fires and no waiting ever appears.
Tokens/cost not in JSONL — observations assert cost/tokens = 0.

sources.note: strings: 'Tool Permission', 'Auto-approve', 'DenyRule', 'requires permission'; --dangerously-skip-permissions flag — confirms the auto-classifying permission system
no path produces a waiting/permission-pending signal; PLANNER_RESPONSE with tool_calls → assistant_message + ToolUse; tool-result → function_call_output (non-settling)
steps 2-3,5-6: PLANNER_RESPONSE(tool_calls) → tool-result, all status DONE, no pending status

preconditions: agy CLI >=1.0.10 on PATH, authenticated via Google OAuth
agy launched so a benign run_command is auto-approved (default mode; the prompt targets a harmless echo)
setup: Fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/; agy mints its own <conv-id>
The tool call MUST be one auto-approved without a prompt — a benign 'echo hello' shell command

script.text: Run the shell command: echo hello — then reply with exactly the word: done

verify: exactly one ready→working transition for the session
at least one tool_use event inside the working span (the run_command fired)
NO state_transition to waiting at any point
exactly one working→ready at turn end; final state ready
